summary: velocityfix postinstall drops and executes Windows PE that exfiltrates host data to 751.lol
details: Package presents itself as a Minecraft Velocity proxy configuration tool but ships an unrelated Windows PE binary (payload.exe) that is executed automatically at install time. package.json declares postinstall="node scripts/loader.js"; loader.js calls exec(`start /b "${payloadPath}"`) to launch the bundled payload.exe during `npm install`. The payload contains sandbox-evasion checks (SbieDll.dll, snxhk.dll, VMware/VirtualBox driver paths), collects host fingerprint data (username, OS info, IP address), packages it into a multipart/form-data body as data.zip, and POSTs it via WinHttp to https://751.lol/upload/. The package's only JS surface (index.js exporting static numeric proxy fields) is a cover story; no Velocity/Minecraft logic exists. Installing this package on a Windows host unconditionally exfiltrates machine identifiers to an attacker-controlled domain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
